title: Protocol Port Quick Reference
when_to_use: Foundational knowledge used in every scanning and enumeration task

steps.title: Web & Remote Access
steps.instruction: Memorise: HTTP=80, HTTPS=443, SSH=22, FTP=21, DNS=53
steps.tip: These 5 ports account for the majority of findings in external assessments

steps.title: Enterprise Services
steps.instruction: Memorise: SMB=445, RDP=3389, SMTP=25, MySQL=3306, MSSQL=1433
steps.tip: SMB and RDP are the most exploited enterprise protocols. Always flag them

steps.title: Transport Protocols
steps.instruction: For each, write the transport protocol (TCP/UDP)
steps.tip: DNS uses both TCP and UDP. Most others are TCP. Know which are which

steps.title: Common Vulnerabilities
steps.instruction: Note one common vulnerability per service (e.g., FTP anonymous login)
steps.tip: HTTP: misconfigs. SSH: brute force. FTP: anonymous login. SMB: EternalBlue. RDP: BlueKeep

steps.title: Create Flashcards
steps.instruction: Create flashcards and test daily until automatic
steps.tip: Use Anki for spaced repetition. Port knowledge should be instant — no thinking required

steps.title: Non-Standard Port Awareness
steps.instruction: Flag any non-standard port usage during scans as suspicious
steps.tip: HTTP on 8080, 8443, or random high ports often indicates development servers or backdoors
